description: The provided image is unreadable and appears as a 'Check for updates' placeholder icon rather than a scientific figure. Based on the accompanying text extraction summary, this figure presumably displays characterization data for three arc-clad Fe-C-Cr-Mn-Si steel validation samples (Sample 1, Sample 2, and Sample 3). The figure likely contains X-ray diffraction (XRD) patterns identifying Sample 1 as single-phase Fe-Cr solid solution with FCC lattice (peaks 100, 200, 211) and Samples 2-3 as Fe-Cr-Ni FCC phases, with peak shifts indicating lattice distortion from atomic size mismatches. It may additionally present Vickers microhardness validation comparing experimental measurements (515 HV, 500 HV, 424 HV) against machine learning predictions (521 HV, 490 HV, 415 HV), demonstrating prediction errors of 1.17%, 2.00%, and 2.09%. These results support the paper's core claim that the CGAN-augmented SVR model with modified solid solution strengthening features achieves extreme high accuracy for steel property prediction.
